title: Iran Domestic/Household Water Consumption and Sector Withdrawal, 1993-2041 (incl. Tehran-specific per-capita figures)
source_org: Fanack Water (Netherlands-based knowledge platform), citing Zekri ed. 2020 Water Policies in MENA Countries (Springer/IWA academic volume); CSIS; Circle of Blue; yekab.com
frequency: irregular (scattered years, thin historical depth -- confirmed genuine data-scarcity for this topic, not a search failure)
units: billion cubic meters per year (sector totals); liters per person per day (per-capita figures)
license_terms: Secondary compilation from a public water-policy knowledge platform, citing an academic edited volume; public-interest journalism (CSIS, Circle of Blue) for Tehran-specific figures
notes: Genuinely thin historical depth compared to the other consumption datasets landed this session -- Iran water-use statistics are dominated by agricultural withdrawal (91% of the 2016 total), and dedicated historical per-capita DOMESTIC/household time series proved hard to find; this is the best available compilation after a real search effort, not a shortcut. No Pahlavi-era water-consumption figures were found at all (flagged as a confirmed gap, not yet searched exhaustively via primary Iranian Ministry of Energy sources which were not reachable this session per the usual Iran-domestic-site access problems documented elsewhere in this project). An NCRI-affiliated article (ncr-iran.org, "Iran Domestic Capacity Crisis: Food, Water, and Urban Safety") surfaced in search results for this topic and was deliberately NOT used, per docs/bookkeeping.md source-reliability policy -- Fanack/CSIS/Circle of Blue were used instead. Every row is individually confidence-flagged (high/medium/low) given the number of secondary/tertiary citations involved for this particular topic.
